result:
I’d make it impossible for people to dehumanize each other.

Not force everyone to agree, not erase conflict, not turn the world into some artificial “nice” place—but remove that mental switch that lets someone see another person as less than fully human. No “they’re animals,” no “they deserve it,” no easy psychological off-ramp from empathy.

Most of the worst things—war crimes, exploitation, cruelty, even everyday indifference—depend on that gap. Close it, and you still get disagreement, competition, even anger… but it’s constrained. People hesitate. They draw lines. They feel the weight of what they’re doing.

It wouldn’t fix everything, but it would quietly reshape almost everything.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
